sound_type: rut roar -- deep sustained bellowing at 80-400 Hz. Combat vocalization during antler contests. Raw, guttural, primal. E=7
ecosystem: River valley, old-growth forest, meadow edges. Fall rut September-October. Peak combat phase.
season: Fall rut September-October. Peak intensity: direct bull-to-bull competition. Antler combat.
family: Cervidae (fifth entry -- deer 195-196, elk bugle 197, elk cow 198)

coordinates.description: Roosevelt Elk on the Olympic Peninsula and Cascades. River valleys and meadow edges during fall rut.

taxonomy_note.description: Third Roosevelt Elk entry. Fifth Cervidae. Elk trio completes (197 bugle, 198 cow call, 199 rut roar). E=7, E=5, E=7 symmetry.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Cervidae: Deer Fawn (195), Deer Buck (196), Elk Bugle (197), Elk Cow (198), Elk Rut Roar (199).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Cervidae: fifth entry. Elk trio complete.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Bull: 300-500 kg. Peak physical condition during rut.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 80-400 Hz. Deepest part of elk vocal range.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Rut roar: 80-400 Hz. Antler clash transient: 1,000-3,000 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Roar: 3-8 sec sustained. Combat: 30 sec to 30 min total.
song_metrics.note_structure: Sustained bellowing. Less structured than bugle. Raw exhalation.
song_metrics.note_types: Rut roar (combat, deep), post-combat roar (victory/exhaustion), combat grunts.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Sustained roar during close-range confrontation. Repeated during antler combat.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, raw, guttural, primal. Chest-resonant. Forced exhalation of aggression.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=7. One of the most physically intense mammalian vocalizations in PNW. Close-range. Sustained.
song_metrics.tempo: Roars during combat are continuous/overlapping. Post-combat roar is single sustained.
song_metrics.context: Fall rut peak (October). Direct bull-to-bull challenge. Antler combat. Harem defense.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Roosevelt Elk rut roar structurally similar across populations.
song_metrics.key_property: RUT ROAR. Deep sustained bellowing. Combat vocalization. ELK TRIO COMPLETE. E=7. E=13 pair with Modest Mouse. v1.49.340.

pelage.overall: Adult bull in rut: dark brown, massive antlers (up to 120 cm span), thickened neck, dark mane. Mud-caked from wallowing. Battle scars.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Massive antlers. Thickened neck. Mud-stained. Aggressive posture. Often with harem of 10-30 cows nearby.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Peak rut condition: maximum neck thickness, fully hardened antlers, aggressive posture. Post-rut: exhausted, weight loss up to 20%.

physical_description.overall: Adult bull in rut: 300-500 kg (700-1,100 lbs). Maximum physical condition. Antlers fully hardened. Neck thickened for combat.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Massive body. Large antlers. Aggressive behavior. Roaring. Often sparring with rival or guarding harem.
physical_description.field_id: Very large brown ungulate with massive antlers, roaring deeply. In meadows or river valleys September-October. Often with group of cows. Do not approach rutting bulls -- they are aggressive and dangerous.

nodes.title: Rut Roar -- Combat Vocalization
nodes.description: Deep sustained bellowing at 80-400 Hz. 3-8 seconds. Close-range direct challenge. The vocal expression of physical contest between bulls.

nodes.title: Antler Combat Sequence
nodes.description: Bugle (long-range) to parallel walk (assessment) to roar (challenge) to antler lock (combat). 30 seconds to 30 minutes. Injuries possible.

nodes.title: Elk Trio Complete -- E=7, E=5, E=7
nodes.description: Symmetrical trio across 197-199. Display (bugle), connection (cow call), combat (rut roar). Full vocal repertoire. Three consecutive degrees.

nodes.title: Harem Dynamics
nodes.description: Winner controls 10-30 cows. Reproductive access. Natural selection mechanism. The rut roar and combat as biological imperative.

cross_references.relationship: ELK TRIO! Bugle (E=7, display) vs. rut roar (E=7, combat). Both maximum energy, different functions.
ELK TRIO! Cow call (E=5) is the essential middle between two E=7 peaks.
CERVIDAE ESCALATION! Deer buck grunt (E=4) to elk rut roar (E=7). Same function, vastly different scale.
RESTLESS POWER! Modest Mouse (E=6) + rut roar (E=7). E=13 combined. Highest pair energy.
